title: ALIEN: ROMULUS Had a Great Opening Weekend with $108.2 Million
text: Director Fede Alvarez's "Alien: Romulus" had a great opening weekend as it scared up $108.2 million in its global box office debut. "Alien: Romulus" earned $41.5 million domestically in its opening weekend making it the second-highest start of the franchise behind Prometheus, which made $51 million. The movie also earned $66.7 million from 49 international territories. David A. Gross, who runs the movie consulting firm Franchise Entertainment Research, said: "A big part of "Alien's" success has been overseas. "Romulus" will follow that pattern, but we also expect the foreign numbers to be off to some degree because several horror movies are doing less foreign business since the pandemic." Blake Lively's "It Ends With Us" came in second and has now made a total of $180 million globally after just 10 days of release. Deadpool & Wolverine came in third and has made a total of $1.14 billion globally. The author expresses enthusiasm about the film's commercial performance, noting that the franchise's continued success suggests potential for future sequels and story expansion.
